text: The adversarial lead car and a static obstacle are placed in sequence ahead of the ego vehicle.
The adversarial pedestrian is on the sidewalk at the corner, positioned to the front-right or front-left of the ego vehicle.
The adversarial car is oncoming from the opposite lane, approaching the intersection from the front.
The adversarial car is in front of the ego.
The adversarial car is in the opposite lane, initially facing the ego vehicle.
Multiple debris props are spawned in a sequence along the ego vehicle's path to create a hazardous road condition
The adversarial pedestrian is positioned behind a parked truck on the side of the road, initially occluded from the ego vehicle's line of sight.
The adversarial pedestrian is initially on the sidewalk or road shoulder, positioned to the front-right or front-left of the ego vehicle.
The adversarial pedestrian is initially positioned on the right, hidden behind a parked car in the ego vehicle's front-right periphery.
The adversarial car is in the lane to the right of the ego vehicle, initially positioned at the rear or side.
The adversarial car is initially in front of the ego vehicle, then becomes adjacent to the ego's side during the bypass attempt.
The adversarial car is in the lateral lane (the "top" of the T or the perpendicular road), approaching from the left.
The adversarial agent is positioned at a specific distance ahead of the ego within the same lane
The adversarial pedestrian is at the corner or on the sidewalk of the destination road, positioned to the front-left of the ego vehicle.
The adversarial car is positioned on a crossing lane that conflicts with the ego's path.
The adversarial car is in the lateral lane (perpendicular to the ego), approaching from either the left or right.
Places the ego and adversary in their respective lanes at specific distances from the intersection to facilitate the merging conflict.
The adversarial pedestrian is on the sidewalk or corner to the front-right of the ego vehicle.
